title: Octomind Cloud Is Live: Real Machines for Your Agent, Every Model Through One Key
excerpt: Your coding agent dies with your laptop lid, your API keys live in five dashboards, and your AI bill is a surprise. We built Octomind Cloud to fix all three: persistent machines from $0.05/hr — with real Docker inside — a hub of 21 models behind one key at published per-token prices, and one wallet with caps you can see.
content_html:
Today Octomind Cloud goes live in beta. Before I tell you what it is, let me tell you the three problems that made us build it — because odds are you have at least two of them right now.

Your agent lives and dies with your laptop. You kick off a long refactor, the agent is forty minutes into it, and then you need to close the lid, catch a train, or your Wi-Fi hiccups. The session is gone, the context is gone, and the tokens you paid for produced work you'll never see. Agents are long-running processes; laptops are not long-running machines.

Your API keys live in five dashboards. OpenRouter for the cheap models, Anthropic for the good ones, OpenAI because some tool demands it, Voyage for embeddings, and a .env file that's one git add -A away from being public. Every provider has its own billing page, its own rate limits, its own "add $5 minimum" — and no single view of what your agent actually spends.

Your AI bill is a mood, not a number. Seat-priced tools give you rate-limit roulette — an opaque allowance that resets on someone else's schedule. Usage-priced tools give you the opposite fear: nothing stops a runaway agent from burning $80 overnight. Neither shows you a per-token price or lets you watch the meter.

Octomind Cloud is our answer to all three. It's two things that share one subscription, one wallet, and one key: the hub (models) and machines (compute). This post is the map of everything that's live today — each section deserves its own deep dive, and those are coming. But this one piece should tell you exactly what you're getting, economics included, because pricing you can't reason about is a bug, not a business model.

The hub: log in once, every model works

The hub is an OpenAI-compatible gateway in front of a curated model roster — one key, every model works, in the panel, in the CLI, in any tool that speaks the OpenAI API:

bash 

curl https://hub.octomind.run/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $OCTOMIND_KEY" \
  -d '{"model": "deepseek-v4-flash", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "hi"}]}'

If you're using the CLI you don't have to handle that key at all. As of 0.38.0, octomind login shows you a short code, opens the panel where you're already signed in, and writes the key to disk itself:

text 

$ octomind login
╭ login · octomind account
│   code  TX8T-UC7U
│   url   https://octomind.run/app/login/cli
│
│ Confirm the code in your browser to finish signing in.
│ waiting…

It's the standard device flow — the same one gh auth login uses — so no password is ever typed into a terminal, each machine gets its own revocable key, and the /usage command tells you what you've spent against your caps without opening the panel.

No provider accounts. No key sprawl. And the part I care most about: published prices, and metering that matches the table exactly.

The roster is deliberately curated, not a 400-model dropdown — twenty-one models that earn their place. The open shelf (DeepSeek V4 Flash and Pro, Qwen3.7 Plus and Max, Kimi 2.6 and 2.7 Code, MiniMax M3, GLM-5.2, Gemma 4) is included in every paid plan and draws from your plan caps. The premium shelf bills per token from prepaid credits, and it's the real thing: Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5; the whole GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) with GPT-5.5, 5.5 Pro and the fast little 5.4 mini and nano; Gemini 3.5 Flash and 3.1 Pro. You see every price before you pick. And there's a free model with a daily allowance that keeps working even when everything else is exhausted, because an agent that goes completely dark the moment you hit a cap is worse than a slow one.

Fair use is per account, not per key: Free runs one model call at a time, Pro two in parallel, Max five, Team fifteen pooled. Mint a thousand keys and the budget doesn't move — and past it, requests queue briefly instead of failing. It's the anti-abuse rule that doubles as a noisy-neighbor guarantee: nobody's runaway script makes your completion wait.

Under the hood this is octohub — open source, Rust, one static binary. The hosted hub is the same code with the keys problem solved. Want to run it yourself with your own provider keys? That's not a loophole — it's the point of publishing it.

Machines: a dev box that exists whether or not your laptop does

A machine is a persistent Linux container in our cloud with the whole agent toolchain baked in — your files, your repos, your indexes, persisted on its own disk. It's got: octomind (the agent), octocode (code indexing + semantic search), octobrain (memory), a web terminal, git, Docker (more on that in a minute), and — on the default image — Node, bun, a headless browser, ffmpeg and image tools. You create one in the panel, it's ready in under a minute, and it's yours.

The billing model is the part I want to defend in detail, because it's where most clouds get either opaque or hostile. A machine is always in one of three states, and the state decides what you pay:

StateWhenSmall (1 vCPU / 2 GB)
ActiveExecuting — tools, code, indexing, embeddings$0.05/hr
Idle-warmSession alive but waiting (including LLM-wait)$0.01/hr
SuspendedNothing running — auto-suspends when idle~$0 (disk only)
ArchivedSuspended 3+ days — parked in cold storage~$0 (extra disk $0.04/GB-mo)

Per-second metering, automatic transitions, you configure nothing. The consequence that matters: while your agent waits on the model — which is most of an agent's wall-clock life — you pay a fifth of the active rate. A five-minute session costs about half a cent, plus $0.001 per session start. 10 GB of working disk comes free with every machine, and sizes scale linearly — Medium (2 vCPU/4 GB) is $0.10/hr, Large (4 vCPU/8 GB) is $0.20/hr. Twice the machine, exactly twice the price; upgrading is never punished. Disk scales with the box the same way — reserve up to 50 GB on a Small, 80 on a Medium, 160 on a Large at $0.10/GB-month, and if you genuinely need more, stretch to double the bundle with the overflow at 2× instead of being told no.

Park a machine long enough — three days suspended — and it quietly moves to cold archive storage: the machine keeps its identity, its files and its place in your account, host resources are freed, and the next start restores it onto any available host in a few minutes instead of seconds. While archived, extra disk above the free 10 GB bills a flat $0.04/GB-month — under every mainstream snapshot price, with no restore fee and no minimum retention — and a default-disk machine parks at exactly $0 for as long as you're subscribed.

Every machine runs under the sysbox runtime in its own user namespace — root inside the machine is nobody on the host. The agent runs as a non-root user on a disk that physically cannot grow past its quota. Secrets go in as write-only encrypted variables: the panel can never show a value back, only a masked hint.

Yes, Docker runs inside — every machine, every plan

Every machine comes up with a real Docker Engine inside, free plan included — nothing to tick, nothing to configure: docker build, compose stacks, testcontainers, all of it, with no --privileged anywhere. The sysbox runtime gives each machine a properly isolated environment where an ordinary dockerd can live without ever seeing the host.

The part we sweated hardest is the accounting, because nested Docker is where clouds usually leak: every byte the inner engine writes — images, layers, volumes — lands on the machine's own capped working disk. df inside the machine, the disk bar in the panel, and the bill all report the same number. Fill the disk and a pull fails with an honest disk-full error, not a surprise invoice; free some space and carry on. Your images and volumes persist across suspend like everything else on the machine.

Sessions that survive you

This is my favorite thing we built, and it's invisible when it works.

Start a turn, then close the tab. Navigate away. Lose your connection in a tunnel. The turn keeps running on the machine, and the relay keeps recording every step — tool calls, output, cost — until it finishes. Open the session from your phone twenty minutes later and the full transcript is there, including everything that happened while nobody was watching. We tested this by killing browsers mid-turn until we couldn't lose a byte.

Nothing was lost.

That screenshot is a real session from while I wrote this post: the agent scaffolded a Bun server, started it, curled /health, and cleaned up after itself. Total cost in the corner: $0.008 — and I navigated away twice in the middle to take the other screenshots in this article. Nothing was lost.

The same session is reachable from the panel, from a plain web terminal, through the developer API (api.octomind.run/api/v1 — create sessions, send messages, tail output over SSE, all scriptable from CI), and through Telegram: one tap connects our bot (or bring your own, if you'd rather own the token), and your machine answers you in chat — real formatting, tables included, not a wall of raw markdown. The agent stops being a browser tab and becomes a thing you own that happens to have several doors. The developer API and the Telegram workflow each deserve a full post of their own — they're coming; this is the overview.

Sessions also remember how they think. Every session starts on your default model and role, and both are switchable per session: pick from the model dropdown in the session header, or type /model gpt-5.6-luna in the chat — the same command works from Telegram and through the API, and the choice persists with the session. Take a premium model for the gnarly refactor, drop back to a cheap one for the cleanup; /role, /effort, /info, /done, /plan, /schedule and /agents ride the same rails from every surface. One detail we're proud of: switching to a model your plan can't call is rejected at switch time with a clear message — not discovered as a cryptic 403 three messages later.

And the agent on the machine is the same octomind you'd run locally — same config format, same sub-agent delegation, same tools. The panel's Config page holds your octomind / octocode / octobrain TOML and bakes it into every machine at creation; model routing and API keys are injected by the cloud, so a config that just says octohub:auto works everywhere without a single key in a dotfile — the hub picks the real model per call purpose (your sessions, the supervisor's checks, compression each get their own), and you retune any of them from the Models page without touching config.

The machine tells the agent where it is

There's a small file with an outsized effect on every machine: AGENTS.md, following the AGENTS.md standard, loaded into every session as opening context. It's how the agent knows the things no model can guess: that /home/octo survives restarts and /tmp doesn't, that the machine auto-suspends and a schedule beats a process left sleeping in the foreground, that you might be reading its answer on a phone with no terminal in sight — so deliverables belong in files it names, not in scrollback you'll never see.

New machines come seeded with our baseline, and it's yours to change at two levels. Edit your account's baseline under Configs → instructions and every machine you create afterwards starts from your version. Or open a machine's Instructions tab and edit the live file — the machine owns its copy from first boot, and your edits survive every restart (a shell works too; it's a real file in the home directory). And because it's the standard rather than something we invented, a repo that carries its own AGENTS.md just works: the agent follows the repo's instructions while working inside it, and the machine's everywhere else.

Embeddings, priced honestly

Embeddings are the highest-volume model call in the whole system — octocode embeds every chunk of every repo it indexes, and re-embeds on change. Which makes them exactly the place where a cloud can quietly do something user-hostile: route everything through a paid API and margin the flood, or double-bill you for compute and tokens on the same operation.

We shipped two lanes instead, and the picker states the deal in plain text:

Local (the default): small open embedding models (fastembed) run on your machine's own CPU. You pay compute — the same $0.05/hr the machine already costs while active — and not a token cent. Indexing a mid-size repo is minutes of CPU, so it's pennies, and the index lands in your account's shared storage where every machine you own reuses it. Index once, search everywhere.

Hub (optional, paid plans): the current embedding generations — the Voyage 4 series, Jina's v5 line, OpenAI's text-embedding-3 — through the same hub key, from $0.02 per million tokens at published list prices, the same table the meter bills from. You'd pick this for quality (voyage-code-3 is genuinely better at code retrieval than anything that fits in 2 GB of RAM) or to keep a Small machine's CPU free while indexing something huge.

Free plan gets the local lane only — hub embeddings proxy paid provider APIs, so they're a paid-plan entitlement, enforced at the key level, not hidden in fine print. Both lanes bill into the same wallet under the same caps as everything else. No new billing concept, no separate embeddings invoice.

One wallet, caps you can actually watch

Everything above — tokens, embeddings, vCPU-seconds, RAM, disk, storage, network — converts to dollars at list price and lands in one wallet with one set of rolling caps you can watch move:

The plans, plainly:

  • Free — $0. $0.15/day of usage on a capable model, the hub + CLI immediately, and 1 Tiny machine (0.5 vCPU / 1 GB, a fixed 5 GB disk) once you have an invite, 2 GB shared storage.

The cap is hard — that's what keeps the free tier free. Free machines auto-suspend when idle and get reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a heads-up email, and never while anything is running; touch it and the clock resets, or upgrade for a machine that stays put.

  • Pro — $20/mo ($10 your first month). Caps of $20/4h · $60/week · $120/month across everything, 3 machine slots (1 slot = 1 vCPU — mix Tiny ½, Small 1, Medium 2 any way that fits), 10 GB shared storage.
  • Max — $100/mo. Caps ×5, 12 machine slots with Large (4 vCPU / 8 GB) unlocked — up to 3 Large at once — 30 GB shared storage.
  • Team — $500/mo. Pooled caps, unlimited members, 60 machine slots, per-member limits so one intern can't drain the pool.

Three design decisions worth calling out. First, caps are total-spend caps, not token caps — compute counts too, so the number in the sidebar is your actual exposure, not one meter of several. Second, credits are the overflow: if you hit a Pro cap mid-task with prepaid credits in the wallet, the session keeps flowing on credits instead of dying at the worst possible moment. The cap protects your budget; it doesn't punish your momentum. A hard stop happens only at zero balance (and on the free tier, by design). Third, machines reserve their runway: a running machine pre-claims what it will burn until each cap window resets — the usage bars draw it as a hatched "reserved" segment next to real spend — so a burst of token calls can never starve a machine mid-task, and a machine your remaining budget can't carry is refused up front instead of dying halfway through.

Every price in this post is on the pricing page and in the panel's Models table, and the meter bills from the same numbers. If you ever catch the meter and the table disagreeing, that's a bug and I want the report.

The whole stack is open source

This launch is a hosted product built from pieces you can already run:

  • octomind — the agent itself, the same binary in every machine
  • octocode — code indexing + semantic search
  • octobrain — persistent memory
  • octohub — the model gateway behind the hub

The cloud is the convenience layer: the machines, the wallet, the one key, the sessions that outlive your laptop. If you'd rather self-host every piece with your own provider keys, the doors are all open and the docs assume you might. We think enough people will prefer the version where somebody else does the plumbing.

What beta means, and what's next

Beta means cloud machines are invite-only while we scale the fleet. We're bootstrapped — no investors — so we buy hardware as people subscribe. We'd rather hand out machines at the rate we can actually serve them than watch a signup rush turn into "at max capacity" for everyone. Three ways in: subscribe and you're in immediately, redeem an invite from someone already here, or use the free tier for a week and earn invites of your own.

The hub and the CLI are open to everyone right now, no invite needed. That's the part that costs us almost nothing to scale, and it's the whole product minus the machine: log in, take a key, and run the open-source agent on your own laptop against every model in the roster.

The hub, your keys, billing and caps are fully live and not going anywhere. Nothing you build on a paid machine is throwaway: disks, shared storage and sessions persist through the beta. (On the free tier, a machine and its shared storage are reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a warning email, never while something's running — so a paid plan is the one that keeps everything until you delete it yourself.)

This post is the wide-angle shot. Over the coming weeks we'll go deep on each piece separately:

  • The developer API — driving machines and sessions from CI and scripts, with SSE streaming
  • Docker inside machines — how sysbox and a capped disk let you run compose with no --privileged and no ghost storage
  • Custom images — bring your own Dockerfile on top of our base, verified and billed transparently
  • Teams in practice — pooled caps, per-member limits, shared machines without shared footguns
  • The embeddings economics — why two lanes, and what indexing a monorepo actually costs
  • Telegram as an agent surface — a machine in your pocket, honestly useful or gimmick?
  • Self-hosting octohub — the same gateway with your own provider keys

If one of those is the thing blocking you today, tell us and we'll bump it up the queue.

Try it

Go to octomind.run/app — login is an email code, no card, no signup step. Or start from the terminal and never touch the panel: install the open-source CLI, run octomind login, confirm the code in your browser, and you're running the same agent locally against the same models, today.

For a cloud machine, you need an invite while we scale — subscribe and it unlocks immediately (Pro is $10 for the first month), or redeem a code if someone passed you one. Use the free tier properly for a week and you'll earn invites to hand out yourself. From "New machine" to a working agent with a browser and a terminal is about a minute.

We've been running our own development on it for weeks — this article's screenshots are my actual account, spending real cents. That's the bar we wanted before calling it a launch: not a demo, a place where your agent works while you don't.

No new billing concept, no separate embeddings invoice.One wallet, caps you can actually watchEverything above — tokens, embeddings, vCPU-seconds, RAM, disk, storage, network — converts to dollars at list price and lands in one wallet with one set of rolling caps you can watch move:The plans, plainly: Free — $0. $0.15/day of usage on a capable model, the hub + CLI immediately, and 1 Tiny machine (0.5 vCPU / 1 GB, a fixed 5 GB disk) once you have an invite, 2 GB shared storage.The cap is hard — that's what keeps the free tier free. Free machines auto-suspend when idle and get reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a heads-up email, and never while anything is running; touch it and the clock resets, or upgrade for a machine that stays put. Pro — $20/mo ($10 your first month). Caps of $20/4h · $60/week · $120/month across everything, 3 machine slots (1 slot = 1 vCPU — mix Tiny ½, Small 1, Medium 2 any way that fits), 10 GB shared storage. Max — $100/mo. Caps ×5, 12 machine slots with Large (4 vCPU / 8 GB) unlocked — up to 3 Large at once — 30 GB shared storage. Team — $500/mo. Pooled caps, unlimited members, 60 machine slots, per-member limits so one intern can't drain the pool.Three design decisions worth calling out. First, caps are total-spend caps, not token caps — compute counts too, so the number in the sidebar is your actual exposure, not one meter of several. Second, credits are the overflow: if you hit a Pro cap mid-task with prepaid credits in the wallet, the session keeps flowing on credits instead of dying at the worst possible moment. The cap protects your budget; it doesn't punish your momentum. A hard stop happens only at zero balance (and on the free tier, by design). Third, machines reserve their runway: a running machine pre-claims what it will burn until each cap window resets — the usage bars draw it as a hatched "reserved" segment next to real spend — so a burst of token calls can never starve a machine mid-task, and a machine your remaining budget can't carry is refused up front instead of dying halfway through.Every price in this post is on the pricing page and in the panel's Models table, and the meter bills from the same numbers. If you ever catch the meter and the table disagreeing, that's a bug and I want the report.The whole stack is open sourceThis launch is a hosted product built from pieces you can already run: octomind — the agent itself, the same binary in every machine octocode — code indexing + semantic search octobrain — persistent memory octohub — the model gateway behind the hubThe cloud is the convenience layer: the machines, the wallet, the one key, the sessions that outlive your laptop. If you'd rather self-host every piece with your own provider keys, the doors are all open and the docs assume you might. We think enough people will prefer the version where somebody else does the plumbing.What beta means, and what's nextBeta means cloud machines are invite-only while we scale the fleet. We're bootstrapped — no investors — so we buy hardware as people subscribe. We'd rather hand out machines at the rate we can actually serve them than watch a signup rush turn into "at max capacity" for everyone. Three ways in: subscribe and you're in immediately, redeem an invite from someone already here, or use the free tier for a week and earn invites of your own.The hub and the CLI are open to everyone right now, no invite needed. That's the part that costs us almost nothing to scale, and it's the whole product minus the machine: log in, take a key, and run the open-source agent on your own laptop against every model in the roster.The hub, your keys, billing and caps are fully live and not going anywhere. Nothing you build on a paid machine is throwaway: disks, shared storage and sessions persist through the beta. (On the free tier, a machine and its shared storage are reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a warning email, never while something's running — so a paid plan is the one that keeps everything until you delete it yourself.)This post is the wide-angle shot. Over the coming weeks we'll go deep on each piece separately: The developer API — driving machines and sessions from CI and scripts, with SSE streaming Docker inside machines — how sysbox and a capped disk let you run compose with no --privileged and no ghost storage Custom images — bring your own Dockerfile on top of our base, verified and billed transparently Teams in practice — pooled caps, per-member limits, shared machines without shared footguns The embeddings economics — why two lanes, and what indexing a monorepo actually costs Telegram as an agent surface — a machine in your pocket, honestly useful or gimmick? Self-hosting octohub — the same gateway with your own provider keysIf one of those is the thing blocking you today, tell us and we'll bump it up the queue.Try itGo to octomind.run/app — login is an email code, no card, no signup step. Or start from the terminal and never touch the panel: install the open-source CLI, run octomind login, confirm the code in your browser, and you're running the same agent locally against the same models, today.For a cloud machine, you need an invite while we scale — subscribe and it unlocks immediately (Pro is $10 for the first month), or redeem a code if someone passed you one. Use the free tier properly for a week and you'll earn invites to hand out yourself. From "New machine" to a working agent with a browser and a terminal is about a minute.We've been running our own development on it for weeks — this article's screenshots are my actual account, spending real cents. That's the bar we wanted before calling it a launch: not a demo, a place where your agent works while you don't.

blocks.html_en: Today Octomind Cloud goes live in beta. Before I tell you what it is, let me tell you the three problems that made us build it — because odds are you have at least two of them right now.

sentences.en: Today Octomind Cloud goes live in beta.
sentences.zh: 今天，Octomind Cloud 测试版上线。

sentences.en: Before I tell you what it is, let me tell you the three problems that made us build it — because odds are you have at least two of them right now.
sentences.zh: 在我告诉你它是什么之前，让我告诉你我们构建它的三个问题 - 因为你现在可能至少有两个问题。

blocks.html_en: Your agent lives and dies with your laptop. You kick off a long refactor, the agent is forty minutes into it, and then you need to close the lid, catch a train, or your Wi-Fi hiccups. The session is gone, the context is gone, and the tokens you paid for produced work you'll never see. Agents are long-running processes; laptops are not long-running machines.

sentences.en: Your agent lives and dies with your laptop.
sentences.zh: 您的经纪人的生死与您的笔记本电脑有关。

sentences.en: You kick off a long refactor, the agent is forty minutes into it, and then you need to close the lid, catch a train, or your Wi-Fi hiccups.
sentences.zh: 你开始了一个漫长的重构，代理花了四十分钟，然后你需要关上盖子，赶火车，或者你的 Wi-Fi 打嗝。

sentences.en: The session is gone, the context is gone, and the tokens you paid for produced work you'll never see.
sentences.zh: 会话消失了，上下文消失了，您为产生的工作支付的代币将永远不会看到。

sentences.en: Agents are long-running processes; laptops are not long-running machines.
sentences.zh: 代理是长时间运行的进程；笔记本电脑不是长时间运行的机器。

blocks.html_en: Your API keys live in five dashboards. OpenRouter for the cheap models, Anthropic for the good ones, OpenAI because some tool demands it, Voyage for embeddings, and a .env file that's one git add -A away from being public. Every provider has its own billing page, its own rate limits, its own "add $5 minimum" — and no single view of what your agent actually spends.

sentences.en: Your API keys live in five dashboards.
sentences.zh: 您的 API 密钥位于五个仪表板中。

sentences.en: OpenRouter for the cheap models, Anthropic for the good ones, OpenAI because some tool demands it, Voyage for embeddings, and a .env file that's one git add -A away from being public.
sentences.zh: OpenRouter 用于便宜的模型，Anthropic 用于好的模型，OpenAI 因为某些工具需要它，Voyage 用于嵌入，以及一个 .env 文件，只需 git add -A 即可公开。

sentences.en: Every provider has its own billing page, its own rate limits, its own "add $5 minimum" — and no single view of what your agent actually spends.
sentences.zh: 每个提供商都有自己的计费页面、自己的费率限制、自己的“最低添加 5 美元”——并且没有单一视图显示您的代理实际支出。

blocks.html_en: Your AI bill is a mood, not a number. Seat-priced tools give you rate-limit roulette — an opaque allowance that resets on someone else's schedule. Usage-priced tools give you the opposite fear: nothing stops a runaway agent from burning $80 overnight. Neither shows you a per-token price or lets you watch the meter.

sentences.en: Your AI bill is a mood, not a number.
sentences.zh: 你的人工智能账单是一种心情，而不是一个数字。

sentences.en: Seat-priced tools give you rate-limit roulette — an opaque allowance that resets on someone else's schedule.
sentences.zh: 座位定价工具为您提供速率限制轮盘赌——一种根据其他人的时间表重置的不透明津贴。

sentences.en: Usage-priced tools give you the opposite fear: nothing stops a runaway agent from burning $80 overnight.
sentences.zh: 按使用量定价的工具会给你带来相反的恐惧：没有什么可以阻止失控的特工一夜之间烧掉 80 美元。

sentences.en: Neither shows you a per-token price or lets you watch the meter.
sentences.zh: 既不向您显示每个代币的价格，也不让您查看仪表。

blocks.html_en: Octomind Cloud is our answer to all three. It's two things that share one subscription, one wallet, and one key: the hub (models) and machines (compute). This post is the map of everything that's live today — each section deserves its own deep dive, and those are coming. But this one piece should tell you exactly what you're getting, economics included, because pricing you can't reason about is a bug, not a business model.

sentences.en: Octomind Cloud is our answer to all three.
sentences.zh: Octomind Cloud 是我们对这三个问题的答案。

sentences.en: It's two things that share one subscription, one wallet, and one key: the hub (models) and machines (compute).
sentences.zh: 有两件事共享一份订阅、一份钱包和一把密钥：集线器（模型）和机器（计算）。

sentences.en: This post is the map of everything that's live today — each section deserves its own deep dive, and those are coming.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章是今天所有内容的地图——每个部分都值得深入探讨，这些即将到来。

sentences.en: But this one piece should tell you exactly what you're getting, economics included, because pricing you can't reason about is a bug, not a business model.
sentences.zh: 但这一篇应该准确地告诉你你得到了什么，包括经济学，因为你无法推理的定价是一个错误，而不是一种商业模式。

sentences.en: The hub: log in once, every model works
sentences.zh: 中心：登录一次，每个模型都可以工作

blocks.html_en: The hub is an OpenAI-compatible gateway in front of a curated model roster — one key, every model works, in the panel, in the CLI, in any tool that speaks the OpenAI API:

sentences.en: The hub is an OpenAI-compatible gateway in front of a curated model roster — one key, every model works, in the panel, in the CLI, in any tool that speaks the OpenAI API:
sentences.zh: 该中心是一个与 OpenAI 兼容的网关，位于精选模型名册前面 - 一键式，每个模型都可以在面板中、CLI 中以及使用 OpenAI API 的任何工具中运行：

blocks.html_en: bash

If you're using the CLI you don't have to handle that key at all. As of 0.38.0, octomind login shows you a short code, opens the panel where you're already signed in, and writes the key to disk itself:

sentences.en: If you're using the CLI you don't have to handle that key at all.
sentences.zh: 如果您使用 CLI，则根本不需要处理该密钥。

sentences.en: As of 0.38.0, octomind login shows you a short code, opens the panel where you're already signed in, and writes the key to disk itself:
sentences.zh: 从 0.38.0 开始，octomind 登录会向您显示一个简短的代码，打开您已登录的面板，并将密钥写入磁盘本身：

blocks.html_en: text

It's the standard device flow — the same one gh auth login uses — so no password is ever typed into a terminal, each machine gets its own revocable key, and the /usage command tells you what you've spent against your caps without opening the panel.

sentences.en: It's the standard device flow — the same one gh auth login uses — so no password is ever typed into a terminal, each machine gets its own revocable key, and the /usage command tells you what you've spent against your caps without opening the panel.
sentences.zh: 这是标准设备流程 - 与 gh auth 登录使用的相同 - 因此无需在终端中输入密码，每台机器都有自己的可撤销密钥，并且 /usage 命令会告诉您在不打开面板的情况下花费了多少资金。

blocks.html_en: No provider accounts. No key sprawl. And the part I care most about: published prices, and metering that matches the table exactly.

sentences.en: No provider accounts.
sentences.zh: 没有提供商帐户。

sentences.en: No key sprawl.
sentences.zh: 没有钥匙蔓延。

sentences.en: And the part I care most about: published prices, and metering that matches the table exactly.
sentences.zh: 而我最关心的部分是：公布的价格，以及与表格完全相符的计量。

blocks.html_en: The roster is deliberately curated, not a 400-model dropdown — twenty-one models that earn their place. The open shelf (DeepSeek V4 Flash and Pro, Qwen3.7 Plus and Max, Kimi 2.6 and 2.7 Code, MiniMax M3, GLM-5.2, Gemma 4) is included in every paid plan and draws from your plan caps. The premium shelf bills per token from prepaid credits, and it's the real thing: Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5; the whole GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) with GPT-5.5, 5.5 Pro and the fast little 5.4 mini and nano; Gemini 3.5 Flash and 3.1 Pro. You see every price before you pick. And there's a free model with a daily allowance that keeps working even when everything else is exhausted, because an agent that goes completely dark the moment you hit a cap is worse than a slow one.

sentences.en: The roster is deliberately curated, not a 400-model dropdown — twenty-one models that earn their place.
sentences.zh: 该名单是经过精心策划的，而不是 400 名模特的下拉菜单——其中 21 名模特赢得了自己的位置。

sentences.en: The open shelf (DeepSeek V4 Flash and Pro, Qwen3.7 Plus and Max, Kimi 2.6 and 2.7 Code, MiniMax M3, GLM-5.2, Gemma 4) is included in every paid plan and draws from your plan caps.
sentences.zh: 开放架（DeepSeek V4 Flash 和 Pro、Qwen3.7 Plus 和 Max、Kimi 2.6 和 2.7 Code、MiniMax M3、GLM-5.2、Gemma 4）包含在每个付费计划中，并从您的计划上限中提取。

sentences.en: The premium shelf bills per token from prepaid credits, and it's the real thing: Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5; the whole GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) with GPT-5.5, 5.5 Pro and the fast little 5.4 mini and nano; Gemini 3.5 Flash and 3.1 Pro.
sentences.zh: 高级货架按预付积分按代币计费，而且这是真实的：Claude Fable 5、Opus 4.8 和 Sonnet 5；整个 GPT-5.6 系列（Sol、Terra、Luna）以及 GPT-5.5、5.5 Pro 和快速的小型 5.4 mini 和 nano； Gemini 3.5 Flash 和 3.1 Pro。

sentences.en: You see every price before you pick.
sentences.zh: 您在选择之前会看到每个价格。

sentences.en: And there's a free model with a daily allowance that keeps working even when everything else is exhausted, because an agent that goes completely dark the moment you hit a cap is worse than a slow one.
sentences.zh: 还有一种每日津贴的免费模型，即使在其他一切都耗尽的情况下也能继续工作，因为一旦达到上限，代理就完全变黑，这比速度慢的代理更糟糕。

blocks.html_en: Fair use is per account, not per key: Free runs one model call at a time, Pro two in parallel, Max five, Team fifteen pooled. Mint a thousand keys and the budget doesn't move — and past it, requests queue briefly instead of failing. It's the anti-abuse rule that doubles as a noisy-neighbor guarantee: nobody's runaway script makes your completion wait.

sentences.en: Fair use is per account, not per key: Free runs one model call at a time, Pro two in parallel, Max five, Team fifteen pooled.
sentences.zh: 合理使用是针对每个帐户，而不是针对每个密钥：一次免费运行一个模型调用，Pro 并行运行两个模型，最多运行 5 个模型，Team 运行 15 个模型。

sentences.en: Mint a thousand keys and the budget doesn't move — and past it, requests queue briefly instead of failing.
sentences.zh: 铸造一千把钥匙，预算不会改变——超过预算后，请求会短暂排队而不是失败。

sentences.en: It's the anti-abuse rule that doubles as a noisy-neighbor guarantee: nobody's runaway script makes your completion wait.
sentences.zh: 这是反滥用规则，同时也是吵闹邻居的保证：没有人的失控脚本让你的完成等待。

blocks.html_en: Under the hood this is octohub — open source, Rust, one static binary. The hosted hub is the same code with the keys problem solved. Want to run it yourself with your own provider keys? That's not a loophole — it's the point of publishing it.

sentences.en: Under the hood this is octohub — open source, Rust, one static binary.
sentences.zh: 在底层，这是 octohub——开源、Rust、一个静态二进制文件。

sentences.en: The hosted hub is the same code with the keys problem solved.
sentences.zh: 托管集线器是相同的代码，但解决了密钥问题。

sentences.en: Want to run it yourself with your own provider keys?
sentences.zh: 想使用自己的提供商密钥自行运行吗？

sentences.en: That's not a loophole — it's the point of publishing it.
sentences.zh: 这不是漏洞——这才是发布它的目的。

blocks.html_en: Machines: a dev box that exists whether or not your laptop does

sentences.en: Machines: a dev box that exists whether or not your laptop does
sentences.zh: 机器：无论您的笔记本电脑是否存在，开发盒都存在

blocks.html_en: A machine is a persistent Linux container in our cloud with the whole agent toolchain baked in — your files, your repos, your indexes, persisted on its own disk. It's got: octomind (the agent), octocode (code indexing + semantic search), octobrain (memory), a web terminal, git, Docker (more on that in a minute), and — on the default image — Node, bun, a headless browser, ffmpeg and image tools. You create one in the panel, it's ready in under a minute, and it's yours.

sentences.en: A machine is a persistent Linux container in our cloud with the whole agent toolchain baked in — your files, your repos, your indexes, persisted on its own disk.
sentences.zh: 机器是我们云中的持久性 Linux 容器，其中包含整个代理工具链 - 您的文件、存储库、索引都保留在其自己的磁盘上。

sentences.en: It's got: octomind (the agent), octocode (code indexing + semantic search), octobrain (memory), a web terminal, git, Docker (more on that in a minute), and — on the default image — Node, bun, a headless browser, ffmpeg and image tools.
sentences.zh: 它有：octomind（代理）、octocode（代码索引+语义搜索）、octobrain（内存）、Web 终端、git、Docker（稍后会详细介绍），以及默认映像上的 Node、bun、无头浏览器、ffmpeg 和映像工具。

sentences.en: You create one in the panel, it's ready in under a minute, and it's yours.
sentences.zh: 您在面板中创建一个，一分钟之内即可准备就绪，然后就属于您了。

blocks.html_en: The billing model is the part I want to defend in detail, because it's where most clouds get either opaque or hostile. A machine is always in one of three states, and the state decides what you pay:

sentences.en: The billing model is the part I want to defend in detail, because it's where most clouds get either opaque or hostile.
sentences.zh: 计费模型是我想要详细辩护的部分，因为这是大多数云变得不透明或敌对的地方。

sentences.en: A machine is always in one of three states, and the state decides what you pay:
sentences.zh: 机器始终处于三种状态之一，状态决定您支付的费用：

sentences.en: Small (1 vCPU / 2 GB)
sentences.zh: 小型（1 个 vCPU/2 GB）

sentences.en: Active
sentences.zh: 积极的

sentences.en: Executing — tools, code, indexing, embeddings
sentences.zh: 执行——工具、代码、索引、嵌入

sentences.en: Idle-warm
sentences.zh: 怠速暖

sentences.en: Session alive but waiting (including LLM-wait)
sentences.zh: 会话处于活动状态但正在等待（包括 LLM 等待）

sentences.en: Suspended
sentences.zh: 暂停

sentences.en: Nothing running — auto-suspends when idle
sentences.zh: 没有运行 - 空闲时自动挂起

sentences.en: ~$0 (disk only)
sentences.zh: ~$0（仅限磁盘）

sentences.en: Archived
sentences.zh: 已存档

sentences.en: Suspended 3+ days — parked in cold storage
sentences.zh: 暂停 3 天以上 — 停放在冷藏库中

sentences.en: ~$0 (extra disk $0.04/GB-mo)
sentences.zh: ~$0（额外磁盘 $0.04/GB-mo）

blocks.html_en: Per-second metering, automatic transitions, you configure nothing. The consequence that matters: while your agent waits on the model — which is most of an agent's wall-clock life — you pay a fifth of the active rate. A five-minute session costs about half a cent, plus $0.001 per session start. 10 GB of working disk comes free with every machine, and sizes scale linearly — Medium (2 vCPU/4 GB) is $0.10/hr, Large (4 vCPU/8 GB) is $0.20/hr. Twice the machine, exactly twice the price; upgrading is never punished. Disk scales with the box the same way — reserve up to 50 GB on a Small, 80 on a Medium, 160 on a Large at $0.10/GB-month, and if you genuinely need more, stretch to double the bundle with the overflow at 2× instead of being told no.

sentences.en: Per-second metering, automatic transitions, you configure nothing.
sentences.zh: 每秒计量，自动转换，您无需配置任何内容。

sentences.en: The consequence that matters: while your agent waits on the model — which is most of an agent's wall-clock life — you pay a fifth of the active rate.
sentences.zh: 重要的后果是：当您的代理等待模型时（这是代理挂钟生活的大部分时间），您只需支付活动费率的五分之一。

sentences.en: A five-minute session costs about half a cent, plus $0.001 per session start.
sentences.zh: 一次五分钟的课程费用约为 0.5 美分，另加每次课程开始费用 0.001 美元。

sentences.en: 10 GB of working disk comes free with every machine, and sizes scale linearly — Medium (2 vCPU/4 GB) is $0.10/hr, Large (4 vCPU/8 GB) is $0.20/hr.
sentences.zh: 每台机器免费提供 10 GB 工作磁盘，并且大小呈线性扩展 — 中型 (2 vCPU/4 GB) 为 0.10 美元/小时，大型 (4 vCPU/8 GB) 为 0.20 美元/小时。

sentences.en: Twice the machine, exactly twice the price; upgrading is never punished.
sentences.zh: 两倍的机器，正好两倍的价格；升级永远不会受到惩罚。

sentences.en: Disk scales with the box the same way — reserve up to 50 GB on a Small, 80 on a Medium, 160 on a Large at $0.10/GB-month, and if you genuinely need more, stretch to double the bundle with the overflow at 2× instead of being told no.
sentences.zh: 磁盘以同样的方式随盒子扩展——小号最多可保留 50 GB，中号可保留 80 GB，大号可保留 160 GB，价格为每月 0.10 美元/GB，如果您确实需要更多，可以以 2 倍的溢出量将捆绑包扩大一倍，而不是被拒绝。

blocks.html_en: Park a machine long enough — three days suspended — and it quietly moves to cold archive storage: the machine keeps its identity, its files and its place in your account, host resources are freed, and the next start restores it onto any available host in a few minutes instead of seconds. While archived, extra disk above the free 10 GB bills a flat $0.04/GB-month — under every mainstream snapshot price, with no restore fee and no minimum retention — and a default-disk machine parks at exactly $0 for as long as you're subscribed.

sentences.en: Park a machine long enough — three days suspended — and it quietly moves to cold archive storage: the machine keeps its identity, its files and its place in your account, host resources are freed, and the next start restores it onto any available host in a few minutes instead of seconds.
sentences.zh: 将一台机器停放足够长的时间（暂停三天），然后它会悄悄地转移到冷存档存储：该机器保留其身份、文件及其在您帐户中的位置，主机资源被释放，下次启动时会在几分钟而不是几秒钟内将其恢复到任何可用主机上。

sentences.en: While archived, extra disk above the free 10 GB bills a flat $0.04/GB-month — under every mainstream snapshot price, with no restore fee and no minimum retention — and a default-disk machine parks at exactly $0 for as long as you're subscribed.
sentences.zh: 存档时，超过免费 10 GB 的额外磁盘每月收取 0.04 美元/GB 的费用（低于每个主流快照价格，没有恢复费用，也没有最低保留费用），只要您订阅，默认磁盘机器的费用正好为 0 美元。

blocks.html_en: Every machine runs under the sysbox runtime in its own user namespace — root inside the machine is nobody on the host. The agent runs as a non-root user on a disk that physically cannot grow past its quota. Secrets go in as write-only encrypted variables: the panel can never show a value back, only a masked hint.

sentences.en: Every machine runs under the sysbox runtime in its own user namespace — root inside the machine is nobody on the host.
sentences.zh: 每台机器都在其自己的用户命名空间中的 sysbox 运行时下运行 - 机器内的 root 是主机上的任何人。

sentences.en: The agent runs as a non-root user on a disk that physically cannot grow past its quota.
sentences.zh: 代理以非根用户身份在物理上无法超出其配额的磁盘上运行。

sentences.en: Secrets go in as write-only encrypted variables: the panel can never show a value back, only a masked hint.
sentences.zh: 秘密作为只写加密变量进入：面板永远无法显示值，只能显示屏蔽提示。

sentences.en: Yes, Docker runs inside — every machine, every plan
sentences.zh: 是的，Docker 在内部运行——每台机器、每一个计划

blocks.html_en: Every machine comes up with a real Docker Engine inside, free plan included — nothing to tick, nothing to configure: docker build, compose stacks, testcontainers, all of it, with no --privileged anywhere. The sysbox runtime gives each machine a properly isolated environment where an ordinary dockerd can live without ever seeing the host.

sentences.en: Every machine comes up with a real Docker Engine inside, free plan included — nothing to tick, nothing to configure: docker build, compose stacks, testcontainers, all of it, with no --privileged anywhere.
sentences.zh: 每台机器内部都有一个真正的 Docker 引擎，包括免费计划——无需勾选，无需配置：docker 构建、组合堆栈、测试容器，所有这些，没有任何地方有特权。

sentences.en: The sysbox runtime gives each machine a properly isolated environment where an ordinary dockerd can live without ever seeing the host.
sentences.zh: sysbox 运行时为每台机器提供了一个适当隔离的环境，普通 dockerd 可以在其中生存而无需看到主机。

blocks.html_en: The part we sweated hardest is the accounting, because nested Docker is where clouds usually leak: every byte the inner engine writes — images, layers, volumes — lands on the machine's own capped working disk. df inside the machine, the disk bar in the panel, and the bill all report the same number. Fill the disk and a pull fails with an honest disk-full error, not a surprise invoice; free some space and carry on. Your images and volumes persist across suspend like everything else on the machine.

sentences.en: The part we sweated hardest is the accounting, because nested Docker is where clouds usually leak: every byte the inner engine writes — images, layers, volumes — lands on the machine's own capped working disk.
sentences.zh: 我们最费劲的部分是会计，因为嵌套 Docker 是云通常泄漏的地方：内部引擎写入的每个字节（图像、层、卷）都会落在机器自己的有上限的工作磁盘上。

sentences.en: df inside the machine, the disk bar in the panel, and the bill all report the same number.
sentences.zh: 机器内部的df、面板中的磁盘栏、账单都报告相同的数字。

sentences.en: Fill the disk and a pull fails with an honest disk-full error, not a surprise invoice; free some space and carry on.
sentences.zh: 填满磁盘，拉取失败，并出现诚实的磁盘已满错误，而不是意外发票；释放一些空间并继续。

sentences.en: Your images and volumes persist across suspend like everything else on the machine.
sentences.zh: 您的图像和卷​​像计算机上的其他所有内容一样在挂起期间持续存在。

sentences.en: Sessions that survive you
sentences.zh: 在您去世后仍然存在的会话

blocks.html_en: This is my favorite thing we built, and it's invisible when it works.

sentences.en: This is my favorite thing we built, and it's invisible when it works.
sentences.zh: 这是我最喜欢的东西，当它工作时它是不可见的。

blocks.html_en: Start a turn, then close the tab. Navigate away. Lose your connection in a tunnel. The turn keeps running on the machine, and the relay keeps recording every step — tool calls, output, cost — until it finishes. Open the session from your phone twenty minutes later and the full transcript is there, including everything that happened while nobody was watching. We tested this by killing browsers mid-turn until we couldn't lose a byte.

sentences.en: Start a turn, then close the tab.
sentences.zh: 开始转动，然后关闭选项卡。

sentences.en: Navigate away.
sentences.zh: 导航离开。

sentences.en: Lose your connection in a tunnel.
sentences.zh: 在隧道中失去连接。

sentences.en: The turn keeps running on the machine, and the relay keeps recording every step — tool calls, output, cost — until it finishes.
sentences.zh: 轮流在机器上持续运行，继电器不断记录每一步——工具调用、输出、成本——直到完成。

sentences.en: Open the session from your phone twenty minutes later and the full transcript is there, including everything that happened while nobody was watching.
sentences.zh: 二十分钟后，通过手机打开会议，完整的文字记录就在那里，包括在没有人观看时发生的所有事情。

sentences.en: We tested this by killing browsers mid-turn until we couldn't lose a byte.
sentences.zh: 我们通过中途终止浏览器来测试这一点，直到我们不能丢失一个字节。

sentences.en: Nothing was lost.
sentences.zh: 什么都没有丢失。

blocks.html_en: That screenshot is a real session from while I wrote this post: the agent scaffolded a Bun server, started it, curled /health, and cleaned up after itself. Total cost in the corner: $0.008 — and I navigated away twice in the middle to take the other screenshots in this article. Nothing was lost.

sentences.en: That screenshot is a real session from while I wrote this post: the agent scaffolded a Bun server, started it, curled /health, and cleaned up after itself.
sentences.zh: 该屏幕截图是我撰写本文时的真实会话：代理搭建了一个 Bun 服务器，启动它，卷曲 /health，然后自行清理。

sentences.en: Total cost in the corner: $0.008 — and I navigated away twice in the middle to take the other screenshots in this article.
sentences.zh: 角落里的总成本：0.008 美元——我在中间两次离开以拍摄本文中的其他屏幕截图。

sentences.en: Nothing was lost.
sentences.zh: 什么都没有丢失。

blocks.html_en: The same session is reachable from the panel, from a plain web terminal, through the developer API (api.octomind.run/api/v1 — create sessions, send messages, tail output over SSE, all scriptable from CI), and through Telegram: one tap connects our bot (or bring your own, if you'd rather own the token), and your machine answers you in chat — real formatting, tables included, not a wall of raw markdown. The agent stops being a browser tab and becomes a thing you own that happens to have several doors. The developer API and the Telegram workflow each deserve a full post of their own — they're coming; this is the overview.

sentences.en: The same session is reachable from the panel, from a plain web terminal, through the developer API (api.octomind.run/api/v1 — create sessions, send messages, tail output over SSE, all scriptable from CI), and through Telegram: one tap connects our bot (or bring your own, if you'd rather own the token), and your machine answers you in chat — real formatting, tables included, not a wall of raw markdown.
sentences.zh: 可以从面板、普通 Web 终端、通过开发人员 API（api.octomind.run/api/v1 — 创建会话、发送消息、通过 SSE 尾部输出，所有可从 CI 编写脚本）以及 Telegram 访问相同的会话：轻轻一按即可连接我们的机器人（如果您想拥有令牌，也可以带上您自己的机器人），您的机器会在聊天中回答您 — 真正的格式，包括表格，而不是一堵原始的 Markdown。

sentences.en: The agent stops being a browser tab and becomes a thing you own that happens to have several doors.
sentences.zh: 代理不再是浏览器选项卡，而是成为您拥有的、碰巧有多个门的东西。

sentences.en: The developer API and the Telegram workflow each deserve a full post of their own — they're coming; this is the overview.
sentences.zh: 开发者 API 和 Telegram 工作流程都值得单独写一篇完整的文章——它们即将到来；这是概述。

blocks.html_en: Sessions also remember how they think. Every session starts on your default model and role, and both are switchable per session: pick from the model dropdown in the session header, or type /model gpt-5.6-luna in the chat — the same command works from Telegram and through the API, and the choice persists with the session. Take a premium model for the gnarly refactor, drop back to a cheap one for the cleanup; /role, /effort, /info, /done, /plan, /schedule and /agents ride the same rails from every surface. One detail we're proud of: switching to a model your plan can't call is rejected at switch time with a clear message — not discovered as a cryptic 403 three messages later.

sentences.en: Sessions also remember how they think.
sentences.zh: 塞申斯还记得他们的想法。

sentences.en: Every session starts on your default model and role, and both are switchable per session: pick from the model dropdown in the session header, or type /model gpt-5.6-luna in the chat — the same command works from Telegram and through the API, and the choice persists with the session.
sentences.zh: 每个会话都以您的默认模型和角色开始，并且每个会话都可以切换：从会话标头中的模型下拉列表中选择，或在聊天中键入 /model gpt-5.6-luna - 相同的命令在 Telegram 和 API 中工作，并且选择在会话中持续存在。

sentences.en: Take a premium model for the gnarly refactor, drop back to a cheap one for the cleanup; /role, /effort, /info, /done, /plan, /schedule and /agents ride the same rails from every surface.
sentences.zh: 采用高级模型进行粗糙的重构，然后使用廉价模型进行清理； /role、/effort、/info、/done、/plan、/schedule 和 /agents 从每个表面都遵循相同的轨道。

sentences.en: One detail we're proud of: switching to a model your plan can't call is rejected at switch time with a clear message — not discovered as a cryptic 403 three messages later.
sentences.zh: 我们引以为傲的一个细节是：切换到您的计划无法调用的模型会在切换时被拒绝，并带有一条明确的消息 - 不会在三条消息后被发现为神秘的 403。

blocks.html_en: And the agent on the machine is the same octomind you'd run locally — same config format, same sub-agent delegation, same tools. The panel's Config page holds your octomind / octocode / octobrain TOML and bakes it into every machine at creation; model routing and API keys are injected by the cloud, so a config that just says octohub:auto works everywhere without a single key in a dotfile — the hub picks the real model per call purpose (your sessions, the supervisor's checks, compression each get their own), and you retune any of them from the Models page without touching config.

sentences.en: And the agent on the machine is the same octomind you'd run locally — same config format, same sub-agent delegation, same tools.
sentences.zh: 机器上的代理与您在本地运行的 octomind 相同 - 相同的配置格式、相同的子代理委托、相同的工具。

sentences.en: The panel's Config page holds your octomind / octocode / octobrain TOML and bakes it into every machine at creation; model routing and API keys are injected by the cloud, so a config that just says octohub:auto works everywhere without a single key in a dotfile — the hub picks the real model per call purpose (your sessions, the supervisor's checks, compression each get their own), and you retune any of them from the Models page without touching config.
sentences.zh: 面板的配置页面保存您的 octomind / octocode / octobrain TOML 并在创建时将其烘焙到每台机器中；模型路由和 API 密钥是由云注入的，因此只需写 octohub:auto 的配置就可以在任何地方工作，而无需在点文件中使用单个密钥 - 集线器根据调用目的选择真正的模型（您的会话、主管的检查、压缩各有自己的），并且您可以从“模型”页面重新调整其中任何模型，而无需触摸配置。

sentences.en: The machine tells the agent where it is
sentences.zh: 机器告诉代理它在哪里

blocks.html_en: There's a small file with an outsized effect on every machine: AGENTS.md, following the AGENTS.md standard, loaded into every session as opening context. It's how the agent knows the things no model can guess: that /home/octo survives restarts and /tmp doesn't, that the machine auto-suspends and a schedule beats a process left sleeping in the foreground, that you might be reading its answer on a phone with no terminal in sight — so deliverables belong in files it names, not in scrollback you'll never see.

sentences.en: There's a small file with an outsized effect on every machine: AGENTS.md, following the AGENTS.md standard, loaded into every session as opening context.
sentences.zh: 有一个小文件，对每台计算机都有巨大的影响：AGENTS.md，遵循 AGENTS.md 标准，作为打开上下文加载到每个会话中。

sentences.en: It's how the agent knows the things no model can guess: that /home/octo survives restarts and /tmp doesn't, that the machine auto-suspends and a schedule beats a process left sleeping in the foreground, that you might be reading its answer on a phone with no terminal in sight — so deliverables belong in files it names, not in scrollback you'll never see.
sentences.zh: 这就是代理如何知道模型无法猜测的事情：/home/octo 在重新启动后仍然存在，而 /tmp 则不能，机器自动挂起，并且时间表击败了在前台休眠的进程，您可能在看不到终端的手机上阅读它的答案 - 因此可交付成果属于它命名的文件，而不是您永远不会看到的回滚。

blocks.html_en: New machines come seeded with our baseline, and it's yours to change at two levels. Edit your account's baseline under Configs → instructions and every machine you create afterwards starts from your version. Or open a machine's Instructions tab and edit the live file — the machine owns its copy from first boot, and your edits survive every restart (a shell works too; it's a real file in the home directory). And because it's the standard rather than something we invented, a repo that carries its own AGENTS.md just works: the agent follows the repo's instructions while working inside it, and the machine's everywhere else.

sentences.en: New machines come seeded with our baseline, and it's yours to change at two levels.
sentences.zh: 新机器带有我们的基线，您可以在两个级别上进行更改。

sentences.en: Edit your account's baseline under Configs → instructions and every machine you create afterwards starts from your version.
sentences.zh: 在“配置”→“说明”下编辑您帐户的基线，之后您创建的每台计算机都将从您的版本开始。

sentences.en: Or open a machine's Instructions tab and edit the live file — the machine owns its copy from first boot, and your edits survive every restart (a shell works too; it's a real file in the home directory).
sentences.zh: 或者打开机器的“指令”选项卡并编辑实时文件 - 机器从第一次启动时就拥有其副本，并且您的编辑在每次重新启动后都会保留（shell 也可以工作；它是主目录中的真实文件）。

sentences.en: And because it's the standard rather than something we invented, a repo that carries its own AGENTS.md just works: the agent follows the repo's instructions while working inside it, and the machine's everywhere else.
sentences.zh: 而且因为它是标准而不是我们发明的东西，所以带有自己的 AGENTS.md 的存储库就可以正常工作：代理在内部工作时遵循存储库的指令，而机器则在其他任何地方。

sentences.en: Embeddings, priced honestly
sentences.zh: 嵌入，诚实定价

blocks.html_en: Embeddings are the highest-volume model call in the whole system — octocode embeds every chunk of every repo it indexes, and re-embeds on change. Which makes them exactly the place where a cloud can quietly do something user-hostile: route everything through a paid API and margin the flood, or double-bill you for compute and tokens on the same operation.

sentences.en: Embeddings are the highest-volume model call in the whole system — octocode embeds every chunk of every repo it indexes, and re-embeds on change.
sentences.zh: 嵌入是整个系统中数量最多的模型调用 - octocode 嵌入它索引的每个存储库的每个块，并在更改时重新嵌入。

sentences.en: Which makes them exactly the place where a cloud can quietly do something user-hostile: route everything through a paid API and margin the flood, or double-bill you for compute and tokens on the same operation.
sentences.zh: 这使得它们正是云可以悄悄地做一些对用户不利的事情的地方：通过付费 API 路由所有内容并保证洪水，或者在同一操作中向您收取计算和令牌的双重费用。

blocks.html_en: We shipped two lanes instead, and the picker states the deal in plain text:

sentences.en: We shipped two lanes instead, and the picker states the deal in plain text:
sentences.zh: 我们改为运送两条路线，拣选员以纯文本形式说明交易：

blocks.html_en: Local (the default): small open embedding models (fastembed) run on your machine's own CPU. You pay compute — the same $0.05/hr the machine already costs while active — and not a token cent. Indexing a mid-size repo is minutes of CPU, so it's pennies, and the index lands in your account's shared storage where every machine you own reuses it. Index once, search everywhere.

sentences.en: Local (the default): small open embedding models (fastembed) run on your machine's own CPU.
sentences.zh: 本地（默认）：小型开放嵌入模型（fastembed）在您机器自己的 CPU 上运行。

sentences.en: You pay compute — the same $0.05/hr the machine already costs while active — and not a token cent.
sentences.zh: 你支付计算费用——与机器运行时的成本相同，每小时 0.05 美元——而不是象征性的一分钱。

sentences.en: Indexing a mid-size repo is minutes of CPU, so it's pennies, and the index lands in your account's shared storage where every machine you own reuses it.
sentences.zh: 为中型存储库建立索引需要几分钟的 CPU 时间，因此只需几便士，并且索引会存放在您帐户的共享存储中，您拥有的每台计算机都可以重复使用它。

sentences.en: Index once, search everywhere.
sentences.zh: 一次索引，到处搜索。

blocks.html_en: Hub (optional, paid plans): the current embedding generations — the Voyage 4 series, Jina's v5 line, OpenAI's text-embedding-3 — through the same hub key, from $0.02 per million tokens at published list prices, the same table the meter bills from. You'd pick this for quality (voyage-code-3 is genuinely better at code retrieval than anything that fits in 2 GB of RAM) or to keep a Small machine's CPU free while indexing something huge.

sentences.en: Hub (optional, paid plans): the current embedding generations — the Voyage 4 series, Jina's v5 line, OpenAI's text-embedding-3 — through the same hub key, from $0.02 per million tokens at published list prices, the same table the meter bills from.
sentences.zh: Hub（可选，付费计划）：当前的嵌入世代 - Voyage 4 系列、Jina 的 v5 系列、OpenAI 的 text-embedding-3 - 通过相同的 hub 密钥，按公布的标价每百万代币 0.02 美元起，与电表计费表相同。

sentences.en: You'd pick this for quality (voyage-code-3 is genuinely better at code retrieval than anything that fits in 2 GB of RAM) or to keep a Small machine's CPU free while indexing something huge.
sentences.zh: 您选择此选项是为了质量（voyage-code-3 在代码检索方面确实比任何适合 2 GB RAM 的东西都要好），或者是为了在索引大型数据时保持小型机器的 CPU 空闲。

blocks.html_en: Free plan gets the local lane only — hub embeddings proxy paid provider APIs, so they're a paid-plan entitlement, enforced at the key level, not hidden in fine print. Both lanes bill into the same wallet under the same caps as everything else. No new billing concept, no separate embeddings invoice.

sentences.en: Free plan gets the local lane only — hub embeddings proxy paid provider APIs, so they're a paid-plan entitlement, enforced at the key level, not hidden in fine print.
sentences.zh: 免费计划仅获得本地通道 - 集线器嵌入代理付费提供商 API，因此它们是付费计划权利，在关键级别强制执行，而不是隐藏在细则中。

sentences.en: Both lanes bill into the same wallet under the same caps as everything else.
sentences.zh: 两条通道的账单都存入同一个钱包，且上限与其他所有账户相同。

sentences.en: No new billing concept, no separate embeddings invoice.
sentences.zh: 没有新的计费概念，没有单独的嵌入发票。

sentences.en: One wallet, caps you can actually watch
sentences.zh: 一个钱包，一个可以实际观看的帽子

sentences.en: Everything above — tokens, embeddings, vCPU-seconds, RAM, disk, storage, network — converts to dollars at list price and lands in one wallet with one set of rolling caps you can watch move:
sentences.zh: 上述所有内容——代币、嵌入、vCPU 秒、RAM、磁盘、存储、网络——都以标价转换为美元，并存入一个钱包中，并带有一组滚动上限，您可以观看移动：

sentences.en: The plans, plainly:
sentences.zh: 计划，简单地说：

sentences.en: Free — $0. $0.15/day of usage on a capable model, the hub + CLI immediately, and 1 Tiny machine (0.5 vCPU / 1 GB, a fixed 5 GB disk) once you have an invite, 2 GB shared storage.
sentences.zh: 免费 — 0 美元。功能强大的型号的使用费为 0.15 美元/天，立即使用集线器 + CLI，一旦收到邀请，即可使用 1 台微型计算机（0.5 vCPU / 1 GB，固定 5 GB 磁盘），2 GB 共享存储。

blocks.html_en: The cap is hard — that's what keeps the free tier free. Free machines auto-suspend when idle and get reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a heads-up email, and never while anything is running; touch it and the clock resets, or upgrade for a machine that stays put.

sentences.en: The cap is hard — that's what keeps the free tier free.
sentences.zh: 上限是硬性的——这就是免费套餐保持免费的原因。

sentences.en: Free machines auto-suspend when idle and get reclaimed after about a week of inactivity — always after a heads-up email, and never while anything is running; touch it and the clock resets, or upgrade for a machine that stays put.
sentences.zh: 免费机器在闲置时自动挂起，并在大约一周不活动后被回收——总是在收到提醒电子邮件后，并且绝不会在任何东西正在运行时；触摸它，时钟就会重置，或者升级为保持原状的机器。

sentences.en: Pro — $20/mo ($10 your first month).
sentences.zh: Pro — 20 美元/月（第一个月 10 美元）。

sentences.en: Caps of $20/4h · $60/week · $120/month across everything, 3 machine slots (1 slot = 1 vCPU — mix Tiny ½, Small 1, Medium 2 any way that fits), 10 GB shared storage.
sentences.zh: 所有内容的上限为 20 美元/4 小时、60 美元/周、120 美元/月、3 个机器插槽（1 个插槽 = 1 个 vCPU — 以任何适合的方式混合 Tiny ½、Small 1、Medium 2）、10 GB 共享存储。

sentences.en: Max — $100/mo.
sentences.zh: 最高 — 100 美元/月。

sentences.en: Caps ×5, 12 machine slots with Large (4 vCPU / 8 GB) unlocked — up to 3 Large at once — 30 GB shared storage.
sentences.zh: Caps × 5，12 个机器插槽，已解锁大型 (4 vCPU/8 GB) — 一次最多 3 个大型 — 30 GB 共享存储。

sentences.en: Team — $500/mo.

blocks.html_en: Three design decisions worth calling out. First, caps are total-spend caps, not token caps — compute counts too, so the number in the sidebar is your actual exposure, not one meter of several. Second, credits are the overflow: if you hit a Pro cap mid-task with prepaid credits in the wallet, the session keeps flowing on credits instead of dying at the worst possible moment. The cap protects your budget; it doesn't punish your momentum. A hard stop happens only at zero balance (and on the free tier, by design). Third, machines reserve their runway: a running machine pre-claims what it will burn until each cap window resets — the usage bars draw it as a hatched "reserved" segment next to real spend — so a burst of token calls can never starve a machine mid-task, and a machine your remaining budget can't carry is refused up front instead of dying halfway through.

sentences.en: Three design decisions worth calling out.

sentences.en: First, caps are total-spend caps, not token caps — compute counts too, so the number in the sidebar is your actual exposure, not one meter of several.

sentences.en: Second, credits are the overflow: if you hit a Pro cap mid-task with prepaid credits in the wallet, the session keeps flowing on credits instead of dying at the worst possible moment.

sentences.en: The cap protects your budget; it doesn't punish your momentum.

sentences.en: Third, machines reserve their runway: a running machine pre-claims what it will burn until each cap window resets — the usage bars draw it as a hatched "reserved" segment next to real spend — so a burst of token calls can never starve a machine mid-task, and a machine your remaining budget can't carry is refused up front instead of dying halfway through.
sentences.zh: 第三，机器保留自己的跑道：正在运行的机器会预先声明它将燃烧的内容，直到每个上限窗口重置为止 - 使用栏将其绘制为实际支出旁边的阴影“保留”部分 - 因此，突发的代币调用永远不会使机器在任务中挨饿，并且您剩余预算无法运行的机器会被预先拒绝，而不是中途死亡。

content_html_zh:
今天，Octomind Cloud 测试版上线。 在我告诉你它是什么之前，让我告诉你我们构建它的三个问题 - 因为你现在可能至少有两个问题。

您的经纪人的生死与您的笔记本电脑有关。 你开始了一个漫长的重构，代理花了四十分钟，然后你需要关上盖子，赶火车，或者你的 Wi-Fi 打嗝。 会话消失了，上下文消失了，您为产生的工作支付的代币将永远不会看到。 代理是长时间运行的进程；笔记本电脑不是长时间运行的机器。

您的 API 密钥位于五个仪表板中。 OpenRouter 用于便宜的模型，Anthropic 用于好的模型，OpenAI 因为某些工具需要它，Voyage 用于嵌入，以及一个 .env 文件，只需 git add -A 即可公开。 每个提供商都有自己的计费页面、自己的费率限制、自己的“最低添加 5 美元”——并且没有单一视图显示您的代理实际支出。

你的人工智能账单是一种心情，而不是一个数字。 座位定价工具为您提供速率限制轮盘赌——一种根据其他人的时间表重置的不透明津贴。 按使用量定价的工具会给你带来相反的恐惧：没有什么可以阻止失控的特工一夜之间烧掉 80 美元。 既不向您显示每个代币的价格，也不让您查看仪表。

Octomind Cloud 是我们对这三个问题的答案。 有两件事共享一份订阅、一份钱包和一把密钥：集线器（模型）和机器（计算）。 这篇文章是今天所有内容的地图——每个部分都值得深入探讨，这些即将到来。 但这一篇应该准确地告诉你你得到了什么，包括经济学，因为你无法推理的定价是一个错误，而不是一种商业模式。

中心：登录一次，每个模型都可以工作

该中心是一个与 OpenAI 兼容的网关，位于精选模型名册前面 - 一键式，每个模型都可以在面板中、CLI 中以及使用 OpenAI API 的任何工具中运行：

bash 

curl https://hub.octomind.run/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $OCTOMIND_KEY" \
  -d '{"model": "deepseek-v4-flash", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "hi"}]}'

如果您使用 CLI，则根本不需要处理该密钥。 从 0.38.0 开始，octomind 登录会向您显示一个简短的代码，打开您已登录的面板，并将密钥写入磁盘本身：

text 

$ octomind login
╭ login · octomind account
│   code  TX8T-UC7U
│   url   https://octomind.run/app/login/cli
│
│ Confirm the code in your browser to finish signing in.
│ waiting…

这是标准设备流程 - 与 gh auth 登录使用的相同 - 因此无需在终端中输入密码，每台机器都有自己的可撤销密钥，并且 /usage 命令会告诉您在不打开面板的情况下花费了多少资金。

没有提供商帐户。 没有钥匙蔓延。 而我最关心的部分是：公布的价格，以及与表格完全相符的计量。

该名单是经过精心策划的，而不是 400 名模特的下拉菜单——其中 21 名模特赢得了自己的位置。 开放架（DeepSeek V4 Flash 和 Pro、Qwen3.7 Plus 和 Max、Kimi 2.6 和 2.7 Code、MiniMax M3、GLM-5.2、Gemma 4）包含在每个付费计划中，并从您的计划上限中提取。 高级货架按预付积分按代币计费，而且这是真实的：Claude Fable 5、Opus 4.8 和 Sonnet 5；整个 GPT-5.6 系列（Sol、Terra、Luna）以及 GPT-5.5、5.5 Pro 和快速的小型 5.4 mini 和 nano； Gemini 3.5 Flash 和 3.1 Pro。 您在选择之前会看到每个价格。 还有一种每日津贴的免费模型，即使在其他一切都耗尽的情况下也能继续工作，因为一旦达到上限，代理就完全变黑，这比速度慢的代理更糟糕。

合理使用是针对每个帐户，而不是针对每个密钥：一次免费运行一个模型调用，Pro 并行运行两个模型，最多运行 5 个模型，Team 运行 15 个模型。 铸造一千把钥匙，预算不会改变——超过预算后，请求会短暂排队而不是失败。 这是反滥用规则，同时也是吵闹邻居的保证：没有人的失控脚本让你的完成等待。

在底层，这是 octohub——开源、Rust、一个静态二进制文件。 托管集线器是相同的代码，但解决了密钥问题。 想使用自己的提供商密钥自行运行吗？ 这不是漏洞——这才是发布它的目的。

机器：无论您的笔记本电脑是否存在，开发盒都存在

机器是我们云中的持久性 Linux 容器，其中包含整个代理工具链 - 您的文件、存储库、索引都保留在其自己的磁盘上。 它有：octomind（代理）、octocode（代码索引+语义搜索）、octobrain（内存）、Web 终端、git、Docker（稍后会详细介绍），以及默认映像上的 Node、bun、无头浏览器、ffmpeg 和映像工具。 您在面板中创建一个，一分钟之内即可准备就绪，然后就属于您了。

计费模型是我想要详细辩护的部分，因为这是大多数云变得不透明或敌对的地方。 机器始终处于三种状态之一，状态决定您支付的费用：

StateWhen小型（1 个 vCPU/2 GB）
积极的执行——工具、代码、索引、嵌入$0.05/hr
怠速暖会话处于活动状态但正在等待（包括 LLM 等待）$0.01/hr
暂停没有运行 - 空闲时自动挂起~$0（仅限磁盘）
已存档暂停 3 天以上 — 停放在冷藏库中~$0（额外磁盘 $0.04/GB-mo）

每秒计量，自动转换，您无需配置任何内容。 重要的后果是：当您的代理等待模型时（这是代理挂钟生活的大部分时间），您只需支付活动费率的五分之一。 一次五分钟的课程费用约为 0.5 美分，另加每次课程开始费用 0.001 美元。 每台机器免费提供 10 GB 工作磁盘，并且大小呈线性扩展 — 中型 (2 vCPU/4 GB) 为 0.10 美元/小时，大型 (4 vCPU/8 GB) 为 0.20 美元/小时。 两倍的机器，正好两倍的价格；升级永远不会受到惩罚。 磁盘以同样的方式随盒子扩展——小号最多可保留 50 GB，中号可保留 80 GB，大号可保留 160 GB，价格为每月 0.10 美元/GB，如果您确实需要更多，可以以 2 倍的溢出量将捆绑包扩大一倍，而不是被拒绝。

将一台机器停放足够长的时间（暂停三天），然后它会悄悄地转移到冷存档存储：该机器保留其身份、文件及其在您帐户中的位置，主机资源被释放，下次启动时会在几分钟而不是几秒钟内将其恢复到任何可用主机上。 存档时，超过免费 10 GB 的额外磁盘每月收取 0.04 美元/GB 的费用（低于每个主流快照价格，没有恢复费用，也没有最低保留费用），只要您订阅，默认磁盘机器的费用正好为 0 美元。

每台机器都在其自己的用户命名空间中的 sysbox 运行时下运行 - 机器内的 root 是主机上的任何人。 代理以非根用户身份在物理上无法超出其配额的磁盘上运行。 秘密作为只写加密变量进入：面板永远无法显示值，只能显示屏蔽提示。

是的，Docker 在内部运行——每台机器、每一个计划

每台机器内部都有一个真正的 Docker 引擎，包括免费计划——无需勾选，无需配置：docker 构建、组合堆栈、测试容器，所有这些，没有任何地方有特权。 sysbox 运行时为每台机器提供了一个适当隔离的环境，普通 dockerd 可以在其中生存而无需看到主机。

我们最费劲的部分是会计，因为嵌套 Docker 是云通常泄漏的地方：内部引擎写入的每个字节（图像、层、卷）都会落在机器自己的有上限的工作磁盘上。 机器内部的df、面板中的磁盘栏、账单都报告相同的数字。 填满磁盘，拉取失败，并出现诚实的磁盘已满错误，而不是意外发票；释放一些空间并继续。 您的图像和卷​​像计算机上的其他所有内容一样在挂起期间持续存在。

在您去世后仍然存在的会话

这是我最喜欢的东西，当它工作时它是不可见的。

开始转动，然后关闭选项卡。 导航离开。 在隧道中失去连接。 轮流在机器上持续运行，继电器不断记录每一步——工具调用、输出、成本——直到完成。 二十分钟后，通过手机打开会议，完整的文字记录就在那里，包括在没有人观看时发生的所有事情。 我们通过中途终止浏览器来测试这一点，直到我们不能丢失一个字节。

什么都没有丢失。

该屏幕截图是我撰写本文时的真实会话：代理搭建了一个 Bun 服务器，启动它，卷曲 /health，然后自行清理。 角落里的总成本：0.008 美元——我在中间两次离开以拍摄本文中的其他屏幕截图。 什么都没有丢失。

可以从面板、普通 Web 终端、通过开发人员 API（api.octomind.run/api/v1 — 创建会话、发送消息、通过 SSE 尾部输出，所有可从 CI 编写脚本）以及 Telegram 访问相同的会话：轻轻一按即可连接我们的机器人（如果您想拥有令牌，也可以带上您自己的机器人），您的机器会在聊天中回答您 — 真正的格式，包括表格，而不是一堵原始的 Markdown。 代理不再是浏览器选项卡，而是成为您拥有的、碰巧有多个门的东西。 开发者 API 和 Telegram 工作流程都值得单独写一篇完整的文章——它们即将到来；这是概述。

塞申斯还记得他们的想法。 每个会话都以您的默认模型和角色开始，并且每个会话都可以切换：从会话标头中的模型下拉列表中选择，或在聊天中键入 /model gpt-5.6-luna - 相同的命令在 Telegram 和 API 中工作，并且选择在会话中持续存在。 采用高级模型进行粗糙的重构，然后使用廉价模型进行清理； /role、/effort、/info、/done、/plan、/schedule 和 /agents 从每个表面都遵循相同的轨道。 我们引以为傲的一个细节是：切换到您的计划无法调用的模型会在切换时被拒绝，并带有一条明确的消息 - 不会在三条消息后被发现为神秘的 403。

机器上的代理与您在本地运行的 octomind 相同 - 相同的配置格式、相同的子代理委托、相同的工具。 面板的配置页面保存您的 octomind / octocode / octobrain TOML 并在创建时将其烘焙到每台机器中；模型路由和 API 密钥是由云注入的，因此只需写 octohub:auto 的配置就可以在任何地方工作，而无需在点文件中使用单个密钥 - 集线器根据调用目的选择真正的模型（您的会话、主管的检查、压缩各有自己的），并且您可以从“模型”页面重新调整其中任何模型，而无需触摸配置。

机器告诉代理它在哪里

有一个小文件，对每台计算机都有巨大的影响：AGENTS.md，遵循 AGENTS.md 标准，作为打开上下文加载到每个会话中。 这就是代理如何知道模型无法猜测的事情：/home/octo 在重新启动后仍然存在，而 /tmp 则不能，机器自动挂起，并且时间表击败了在前台休眠的进程，您可能在看不到终端的手机上阅读它的答案 - 因此可交付成果属于它命名的文件，而不是您永远不会看到的回滚。

新机器带有我们的基线，您可以在两个级别上进行更改。 在“配置”→“说明”下编辑您帐户的基线，之后您创建的每台计算机都将从您的版本开始。 或者打开机器的“指令”选项卡并编辑实时文件 - 机器从第一次启动时就拥有其副本，并且您的编辑在每次重新启动后都会保留（shell 也可以工作；它是主目录中的真实文件）。 而且因为它是标准而不是我们发明的东西，所以带有自己的 AGENTS.md 的存储库就可以正常工作：代理在内部工作时遵循存储库的指令，而机器则在其他任何地方。

嵌入，诚实定价

嵌入是整个系统中数量最多的模型调用 - octocode 嵌入它索引的每个存储库的每个块，并在更改时重新嵌入。 这使得它们正是云可以悄悄地做一些对用户不利的事情的地方：通过付费 API 路由所有内容并保证洪水，或者在同一操作中向您收取计算和令牌的双重费用。

我们改为运送两条路线，拣选员以纯文本形式说明交易：

本地（默认）：小型开放嵌入模型（fastembed）在您机器自己的 CPU 上运行。 你支付计算费用——与机器运行时的成本相同，每小时 0.05 美元——而不是象征性的一分钱。 为中型存储库建立索引需要几分钟的 CPU 时间，因此只需几便士，并且索引会存放在您帐户的共享存储中，您拥有的每台计算机都可以重复使用它。 一次索引，到处搜索。

Hub（可选，付费计划）：当前的嵌入世代 - Voyage 4 系列、Jina 的 v5 系列、OpenAI 的 text-embedding-3 - 通过相同的 hub 密钥，按公布的标价每百万代币 0.02 美元起，与电表计费表相同。 您选择此选项是为了质量（voyage-code-3 在代码检索方面确实比任何适合 2 GB RAM 的东西都要好），或者是为了在索引大型数据时保持小型机器的 CPU 空闲。

免费计划仅获得本地通道 - 集线器嵌入代理付费提供商 API，因此它们是付费计划权利，在关键级别强制执行，而不是隐藏在细则中。 两条通道的账单都存入同一个钱包，且上限与其他所有账户相同。 没有新的计费概念，没有单独的嵌入发票。

一个钱包，一个可以实际观看的帽子

上述所有内容——代币、嵌入、vCPU 秒、RAM、磁盘、存储、网络——都以标价转换为美元，并存入一个钱包中，并带有一组滚动上限，您可以观看移动：

计划，简单地说：

  • 免费 — 0 美元。功能强大的型号的使用费为 0.15 美元/天，立即使用集线器 + CLI，一旦收到邀请，即可使用 1 台微型计算机（0.5 vCPU / 1 GB，固定 5 GB 磁盘），2 GB 共享存储。

上限是硬性的——这就是免费套餐保持免费的原因。 免费机器在闲置时自动挂起，并在大约一周不活动后被回收——总是在收到提醒电子邮件后，并且绝不会在任何东西正在运行时；触摸它，时钟就会重置，或者升级为保持原状的机器。

  • Pro — 20 美元/月（第一个月 10 美元）。 所有内容的上限为 20 美元/4 小时、60 美元/周、120 美元/月、3 个机器插槽（1 个插槽 = 1 个 vCPU — 以任何适合的方式混合 Tiny ½、Small 1、Medium 2）、10 GB 共享存储。
  • 最高 — 100 美元/月。 Caps × 5，12 个机器插槽，已解锁大型 (4 vCPU/8 GB) — 一次最多 3 个大型 — 30 GB 共享存储。
  • 团队——500 美元/月。 池化上限、无限成员、60 个机器槽位、每个成员的限制，因此一名实习生无法耗尽池中的资源。

值得一提的三个设计决策。 首先，上限是总支出上限，而不是代币上限 - 也计算计数，因此侧栏中的数字是您的实际暴露量，而不是一米的几米。 其次，积分是溢出的：如果你在任务中达到了专业上限，并在钱包中预付了积分，那么会话将继续使用积分，而不是在最糟糕的时刻死亡。 上限可以保护您的预算；它不会惩罚你的动力。 硬停止仅在零余额时发生（并且根据设计，在免费套餐上）。 第三，机器保留自己的跑道：正在运行的机器会预先声明它将燃烧的内容，直到每个上限窗口重置为止 - 使用栏将其绘制为实际支出旁边的阴影“保留”部分 - 因此，突发的代币调用永远不会使机器在任务中挨饿，并且您剩余预算无法运行的机器会被预先拒绝，而不是中途死亡。

整个堆栈都是开源的

此次发布是一个托管产品，由您已经可以运行的部分构建：

  • octomind — 代理本身，每台机器上都有相同的二进制文件
  • octocode — 代码索引 + 语义搜索
  • octobrain——持久记忆
  • octohub — 集线器背后的模型网关

云是便利层：机器、钱包、一键、比笔记本电脑更长寿的会话。 如果您希望使用自己的提供商密钥自行托管每个部分，那么大门都是敞开的，并且文档假设您可以这样做。 我们认为有足够多的人会更喜欢由其他人负责管道的版本。

Beta 意味着什么以及下一步是什么

Beta 意味着在我们扩展机群时，云计算机仅受邀请。 我们是自力更生的——没有投资者——所以我们随着人们订阅而购买硬件。 我们宁愿按照我们实际可以提供服务的速度分发机器，也不愿看着注册热潮变成每个人的“最大容量”。 三种加入方式：订阅即可立即加入，兑换来自已在此的某人的邀请，或使用免费套餐一周并获得自己的邀请。

该中心和 CLI 目前向所有人开放，无需邀请。 这是我们几乎不需要花费任何成本来扩展的部分，它是整个产品减去机器：登录，获取密钥，然后在您自己的笔记本电脑上针对名册中的每个模型运行开源代理。

集线器、您的密钥、计费和上限均完全可用，不会去任何地方。 在付费机器上构建的任何东西都不是一次性的：磁盘、共享存储和会话在测试版中持续存在。 （在免费套餐中，机器及其共享存储在大约一周不活动后会被回收 - 总是在警告电子邮件之后，而不会在某些东西正在运行时 - 因此付费计划可以保留所有内容，直到您自己删除它为止。）
